Class LibraryMigrationService

java.lang.Object
com.sebastiandorata.musicdashboard.service.LibraryMigrationService
@Service public class LibraryMigrationService extends Object
Scans a user-selected folder and reconciles the audio files it contains against the songs already stored in the database.

What this service does

  1. Recursively collects every supported audio file (.mp3, .m4a) from the chosen folder.
  2. For each file, attempts to find a matching Song record using two strategies in order:
    1. Exact path — the file's absolute path already matches a DB row → no path change needed, but cover art may still be refreshed.
    2. Metadata fingerprint — the file's title + primary artist (read from the audio tag) match an existing song → the stored path is updated to the new location.
  3. If cover art is missing for the matched album, it is re-extracted from the audio file's embedded artwork and saved as cover.jpg in the file's parent folder.
  4. No data is ever deleted. Artist, album, genre, playback history, favourites, and playlist associations are all preserved unconditionally.
  5. Files that cannot be matched to any existing song are skipped — they are not imported as new songs. Use the normal Import page for that.

Thread safety

The public entry point scanFolder(File, LibraryMigrationService.TriConsumer) is designed to be called from a background thread (via DataLoadingService). All database writes are wrapped in a @Transactional helper so each file is committed independently; a failure on one file does not roll back updates already applied to others.

Time Complexity: O(f * s) where f = files in folder, s = songs in DB.
Space Complexity: O(f) for the result list.

  • Constructor Details

    • LibraryMigrationService

      public LibraryMigrationService()

  • Method Details

    • scanFolder

      public List<MigrationResult> scanFolder(File rootFolder, LibraryMigrationService.TriConsumer<Integer,Integer,String> onFileProcessed)
      Recursively scans rootFolder, matches each audio file to an existing DB song, updates stale paths, and refreshes missing cover art.

      This method is blocking and is intended to run on a background thread. Progress callbacks are fired on the calling thread; the caller is responsible for dispatching UI updates to the JavaFX thread via Platform.runLater(Runnable) if needed.

      Parameters:
      rootFolder - the folder to scan (searched recursively)
      onFileProcessed - callback fired after each file is evaluated; receives (filesProcessedSoFar, totalFiles, currentFileName)
      Returns:
      a list of MigrationResult — one entry per audio file found

    • syncMetadata

      @Transactional protected List<String> syncMetadata(Song song, org.jaudiotagger.audio.AudioFile audioFile, org.jaudiotagger.tag.Tag tag, File sourceFile, boolean updatePath)
      Reads every relevant tag field from audioFile and writes any changed values back to the Song (and its Album) in the database. The file path is always updated when updatePath is true.

      Fields synced on the Song entity

      • filePath (when updatePath is true)
      • title — only if the tag has a non-blank value different from what is stored
      • duration — re-read from the audio header (always authoritative)
      • trackNumber — parsed from the TRACK tag ("5" or "5/12")
      • bitRate, sampleRate, channels, codec, fileFormat, fileSizeBytes — from audio header
      • artists — resolved via ArtistRepository; new names are created, removed names are unlinked (the Artist row itself is never deleted)
      • genres — resolved via GenreRepository; same create-or-reuse rule

      Fields synced on the Album entity

      • releaseYear — from YEAR tag; only written if currently null or different
      • albumArtPath — written if missing or the file on disk no longer exists

      Playback history, favourites, and playlist associations are never modified.

      Parameters:
      song - the managed Song entity to update
      audioFile - the jAudioTagger handle for the physical file
      tag - the tag read from audioFile (may be null for untagged files)
      sourceFile - the physical audio file on disk
      updatePath - when true the song's filePath is set to sourceFile.getAbsolutePath()
      Returns:
      a human-readable list of field names that were actually changed, empty when everything was already up to date

    • maybeRefreshArt

      @Transactional protected boolean maybeRefreshArt(Song song, org.jaudiotagger.audio.AudioFile audioFile, File sourceFile)
      Re-extracts embedded artwork from audioFile and saves it as cover.jpg if the song's album has no art path recorded yet.
      Returns:
      true if art was written to disk and the album was updated
